definition: A binding factor that ties one to continued becoming and blocks full release.
notes: The project keeps fetter as the default because it is the clearest concise English label for the canonical set and already functions well in stage-of-awakening contexts. Bond and binding fetter remain explanatory alternates when the tying function needs to be heard more explicitly. Saṃyojana should not be flattened into vague attachment-language: in this repository it belongs to the bondage layer that binds one into continued becoming. That distinguishes it from anusaya, which may lie latent, from nīvaraṇa, which actively obstructs practice, and from the broader kilesa family. Formulaic and list contexts such as the lower and higher fetters should stay tightly governed by the headword rather than paraphrased afresh each time. The same policy extends by default into named compounds such as orambhāgiya-saṃyojana and uddhambhāgiya-saṃyojana unless a narrower override is recorded. The OSF glossary supports keeping fetter as the house default, and MN 64 supports the lower-fetter and higher-fetter classifications.

context_rules.context: In doctrinal lists and stage-of-awakening analysis
In explanatory prose emphasizing the tying or binding function
context_rules.notes: Allowed alternate when the binding image matters more than the familiar doctrinal label.

context_rules.context: In translator-facing prose that needs both the technical label and the function
context_rules.notes: Use selectively, not as the normal running translation.

context_rules.context: In lower-fetter and higher-fetter subclass lists

example_phrases.notes: General plural reference to the canonical binding set.
Shows a standard sub-classification of the fetters.
Shows the complementary upper sub-classification used in awakening-stage analysis.

authority_basis.scope: Supports keeping fetter as the house default while allowing bond as a controlled explanatory alternate.
Supports the standard lower-fetter and higher-fetter classifications.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use 'fetter' as the default rendering in the entry's governed doctrinal and stage-of-awakening contexts unless a recorded context rule overrides it.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Use 'bond' or 'binding fetter' only when the local prose needs the tying function made explicit. Do not flatten saṃyojana into generic attachment-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between binding fetter-language, latent-tendency language, active hindrance language, and generic attachment-language.
